name: tawk.to API
what_it_does: Free live chat platform REST API for managing conversations, contacts, and chat history for customer support automation.
use_cases: Retrieve chat transcripts and conversation history for agent training data pipelines
Create and update contact records programmatically from CRM integration workflows
Build automated ticket routing by querying active chat conversations and agent availability
Extract customer support analytics (response times, conversation counts) for reporting agents
Automate pre-chat contact creation to pre-populate agent context before human handoff
not_for: Sending messages from the API — tawk.to REST API is read/management only, not send-message
Real-time bot automation within chat sessions — use tawk.to JavaScript API for in-widget automation
High-volume data exports — tawk.to throttles heavy API usage aggressively
best_when: Best for reading tawk.to chat history and managing contacts from backend automation workflows.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need to send messages or build chatbots via API — use tawk.to's JavaScript widget API instead.

auth.notes: REST API uses API key (base64 encoded with colon prefix) in Authorization header. Property ID required in all requests.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — no paid tiers for core live chat
pricing.paid_tiers: Hired Agents service available for outsourced chat staffing
pricing.notes: tawk.to is free forever for live chat. Revenue comes from hired agent services.

performance.rate_limits: Undocumented but enforced — aggressive throttling on bulk requests

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Minimal error detail — mostly HTTP status codes without actionable messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET endpoints are idempotent; write operations are not.
known_agent_gotchas: REST API is management-only — there is no API endpoint to send a chat message to a visitor
Property ID and Account ID are different identifiers — using the wrong one in API calls returns 404 or empty results
Rate limits are undocumented and silently enforced — implement 1-2 second delays between bulk requests to avoid throttling
Webhook events use different field names than REST API responses — don't assume schema parity between webhook payloads and API data
API documentation is sparse and outdated — test in Postman before relying on docs for field names and types

security_score_components.security_notes: API key only — no scopes or fine-grained access control. Key rotation not automated.
